title: celtis fluminensis
intro: Celtis fluminensis, also known as the Rio Grande cottonwood or Rio Grande hackberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cannabaceae, commonly found in the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. Celtis fluminensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
